dataset: Bahmni/OpenMRS 1.0 Lite with CIEL concept dictionary, programmatically seeded patient data

pi_items.source: Live environment API query: GET /patient?identifier=BAH000030 returned patient with display='BAH000030 - Amina Nyong’o', UUID=9cba9f7e-f0a0-49f9-a283-0dec03390915. Confirmed in evidence file 02_patient_data_raw.json. Identifier is unique and not used by any seeded patient (BAH000002-BAH000021).
Live API response: person.preferredName.givenName='Amina' in 02_patient_data_raw.json. Setup script line 55: GIVEN_NAME='Amina'. Patient creation payload uses givenName='Amina'.

Live API response: person.preferredName.familyName="Nyong'o" in 02_patient_data_raw.json. Setup script line 56: FAMILY_NAME="Nyong'o". Note the apostrophe in the name, which is correctly handled by the API.

Live API response: GET /obs?patient=... returned observation with concept 5089AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA (Weight kg), value=185.0. Confirmed in evidence file 09_final_observations.json: display='Weight (kg): 185.0'. Originally designed as 680kg but OpenMRS CIEL Weight concept has hiAbsolute=250, so 185kg was used instead (above normal range ~120kg but within OpenMRS limit). Setup script line 263: (WEIGHT_UUID, 185, 'weight-185kg-ERRONEOUS').

This is a task target value (what the agent should enter), not pre-seeded data. Physiologically verified: BMI = 68/(1.63^2) = 25.6 (normal/slightly overweight range for a 47-year-old female, 163cm tall). The value 68 is well within OpenMRS Weight concept limits (lowAbsolute=0, hiAbsolute=250). The erroneous value 185 is clearly distinguishable from the correct 68.

Task target value. Clinically verified: 38.7C is febrile (normal <38C), consistent with community-acquired pneumonia and SIRS criteria (temp >38C or <36C). CIEL concept 5088AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA ('Temperature (c)') confirmed present in Bahmni Lite DB via MySQL query (evidence file 18_vital_concepts_db.txt line 13). Value within OpenMRS acceptable range.

Task target value. Clinically verified: 24/min is tachypneic (normal 12-20/min), consistent with pneumonia and SIRS criteria (RR >20). CIEL concept 5242AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA ('Respiratory rate') confirmed present in Bahmni Lite DB via MySQL query (evidence file 18_vital_concepts_db.txt line 17). OpenMRS CIEL dictionary (rwanda.pih-emr.org/openmrs/dictionary/concept.htm?conceptId=5242) confirms concept exists.

Task target value. Clinically verified: SpO2 93% is hypoxic (normal 95-100%), consistent with pneumonia causing impaired gas exchange. CIEL concept 5092AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA ('Arterial blood oxygen saturation (pulse oximeter)') confirmed present in Bahmni Lite DB via MySQL query (evidence file 18_vital_concepts_db.txt line 14). OpenMRS Talk discussion (talk.openmrs.org/t/ciel-proposal-changing-spo2-and-other-vital-sign-concept-types/34555) confirms CIEL:5092 is SpO2.

Task target value. Clinically verified: systolic 100 mmHg is hypotensive (normal >120), consistent with sepsis-induced hemodynamic instability. CIEL concept 5085AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA ('Systolic blood pressure') confirmed in DB (evidence file 18_vital_concepts_db.txt line 3).

Task target value. Clinically verified: diastolic 65 mmHg is low (normal 60-80), consistent with sepsis. Mean arterial pressure = (100 + 2*65)/3 = 76.7 mmHg, borderline for adequate organ perfusion (<65 is critical). CIEL concept 5086AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA ('Diastolic blood pressure') confirmed in DB (evidence file 18_vital_concepts_db.txt line 6).

Live API confirmed: GET /order?patient=...&t=drugorder returned active order with drug.display='Amoxicillin 500mg', concept.display='Amoxicillin', concept.uuid=71160AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA (CIEL standard Amoxicillin). Evidence file 10_final_drug_orders.json shows: action='NEW', dateStopped=null (active), dosingInstructions='500mg three times daily for 7 days'. Amoxicillin IS a penicillin-class antibiotic (aminopenicillin) — confirmed contraindicated for penicillin-allergic patients per IDSA CAP guidelines and multiple PMC references (PMC8355153, PMC6031596).

Live API confirmed: GET /patient/{uuid}/allergy returned allergy with display='Penicillin', allergen.codedAllergen.uuid=81724AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA, which has SNOMED-CT mapping 373270004 (Penicillin substance) and RxNORM mapping 70618. Evidence file 11_final_allergies.json shows this in full. The allergy was created using the CIEL coded concept, not freetext.

Clinically verified against IDSA/ATS CAP guidelines and Stanford SHC Clinical Pathway (med.stanford.edu Pneumonia-Guideline.pdf). For penicillin-allergic patients with CAP: respiratory fluoroquinolones (levofloxacin, moxifloxacin) and macrolides (azithromycin, clarithromycin) are first-line alternatives. Doxycycline is recommended as alternative in ATS/IDSA 2019 guidelines. Vancomycin, linezolid are used for MRSA coverage. Meropenem (carbapenem) has <1% cross-reactivity with penicillin per PMC literature (PMC2528726). Metronidazole has no beta-lactam structure. Clindamycin is a lincosamide, no cross-reactivity. All 14 drugs are non-penicillin, non-cephalosporin antibiotics appropriate for penicillin-allergic patients. Two of these (Azithromycin 500mg, Levofloxacin 500mg) were confirmed available in the Bahmni drug catalog via live environment testing (evidence file 12_drug_catalog.json).

Pharmacologically verified: First 4 (amoxicillin, ampicillin, penicillin, piperacillin) are penicillin-class antibiotics — directly contraindicated. Last 6 (cephalexin=1st-gen, cefazolin=1st-gen, ceftriaxone=3rd-gen, cefuroxime=2nd-gen, cefixime=3rd-gen, cefotaxime=3rd-gen) are cephalosporins with beta-lactam ring cross-reactivity. Per PubMed (PMID:21742459) and PMC6789778: first-generation cephalosporins (cephalexin, cefazolin) have highest cross-reactivity with penicillins due to shared R1 side chains. Third-gen cephalosporins have ~1% cross-reactivity but are still avoided in severe/anaphylactic penicillin allergy per clinical guidelines. Note: the blacklist is conservative (includes 3rd-gen cephalosporins) which is appropriate for a patient safety scenario where the allergy severity is unknown.

OpenMRS/Bahmni diagnosis system supports PRIMARY/SECONDARY order and CONFIRMED/PRESUMED certainty — confirmed in Bahmni source code (openmrs-module-bahmniapps locale_en.json) and validated_pi.json of the existing record_diagnosis task. 'Pneumonia' is a standard CIEL diagnosis concept (confirmed in CIEL dictionary). The clinical picture (febrile 38.7C, tachypneic RR 24, hypoxic SpO2 93%, hypotensive BP 100/65) is consistent with confirmed pneumonia. PRIMARY is correct as the presenting diagnosis.

Sepsis is a standard CIEL diagnosis concept. SECONDARY order is correct (sepsis is secondary to pneumonia). PRESUMED certainty is clinically appropriate — sepsis diagnosis requires meeting qSOFA/SIRS criteria (this patient meets >=2: RR>22, systolic<100), but definitive sepsis confirmation requires blood culture results which are not yet available in this scenario. Per Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines, 'presumed' is the appropriate certainty pending cultures.

Bahmni Wiki (bahmni.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BAH/pages/50921523/Configure+Disposition+Concepts) confirms 'Admit Patient' is a standard Bahmni disposition concept. The disposition system requires a concept named 'Disposition' with coded answers including 'Admit Patient'. This is pre-configured in the Bahmni 1.0 clinic-config Docker image.

Task requirement for scheduling. 7-day follow-up is clinically standard for post-hospitalization pneumonia follow-up (ATS/IDSA guidelines recommend follow-up within 1-2 weeks). The Bahmni Appointments module was confirmed functional in live testing — the existing appointment_schedule_audit task uses the same module successfully. Export script queries appointments in a 5-9 day window from task date to verify.

Arbitrary benchmark threshold. 200 characters is approximately 2-3 sentences. This is a task design parameter, not verifiable against external data. For reference, the existing chronic_disease_followup task requires >=100 chars and inpatient_admission_workflow requires >=150 chars. 200 is a modest increase consistent with the higher complexity of this task.

privileged_info_summary: Patient Amina Nyong'o (BAH000030) is a 47-year-old female (DOB 1978-08-14) created by the setup script with identifier BAH000030. The patient has a pre-seeded active OPD visit containing a consultation encounter with three observations: Weight 185.0 kg (erroneous — the correct weight is 68 kg), Height 163.0 cm, and Pulse 102.0 bpm. Four vitals are MISSING and must be added: temperature 38.7 C (CIEL:5088), respiratory rate 24/min (CIEL:5242), SpO2 93% (CIEL:5092), blood pressure 100/65 mmHg (CIEL:5085 systolic, CIEL:5086 diastolic). The patient has a documented coded Penicillin allergy (CIEL concept 81724, SNOMED-CT 373270004). Two active drug orders exist: Amoxicillin 500mg (CONTRAINDICATED due to penicillin allergy — must be discontinued) and Paracetamol 1g (correct, should remain). Safe alternative antibiotics available in the drug catalog include Azithromycin 500mg and Levofloxacin 500mg. The agent must NOT prescribe any penicillin (amoxicillin, ampicillin, penicillin, piperacillin) or cephalosporin (cephalexin, cefazolin, ceftriaxone, cefuroxime, cefixime, cefotaxime). Two diagnoses must be recorded: Pneumonia (PRIMARY, CONFIRMED) and Sepsis (SECONDARY, PRESUMED). Disposition must be set to 'Admit Patient' with ICU note. A follow-up appointment must be scheduled approximately 7 days from today. A clinical note of at least 200 characters must be written. Login credentials are superman/Admin123. The Bahmni system runs at https://localhost with self-signed SSL certificate.
